creator: asomers@FreeBSD.org
text:
There is another symptom of this bug: due to an assumption in ls(1) that all files with the same inode will have the same value of _PC_ACL_NFS4, ls will sometimes print "Operation not supported" for entries in the ctldir.  Whether or not it does depends on the order that it prints the entries, but you can always demonstrate it by sorting the entries in both forward and reverse order:

$ ls -ltar /tmp/.zfs
total 145
dr-xr-xr-x   3 root  wheel   3 Dec  9  2015 .
dr-xr-xr-x   2 root  wheel   2 Feb 13 13:21 snapshot
drwxrwxrwt  10 root  wheel  10 Feb 13 17:10 ..
$ ls -lta /tmp/.zfs
total 145
drwxrwxrwt  10 root  wheel  10 Feb 13 17:10 ..
ls: /tmp/.zfs/snapshot: Operation not supported
dr-xr-xr-x   2 root  wheel   2 Feb 13 13:21 snapshot
ls: /tmp/.zfs/.: Operation not supported
dr-xr-xr-x   3 root  wheel   3 Dec  9  2015 .
